_note: Two SEPARATE king-list islands, each its own succession chain with no bloodline link to Adam's line or to each other. (A) THE KINGS OF ELAM (ids prefixed EL_) — the Awan dynasty (c.2400–2100 BC) then the Shimashki dynasty (c.2037–1970 BC) of ancient Iran, from the Susa dynastic king-list. Elam is named in Genesis 10:22 as a son of Shem, but no documented descent connects these historical rulers to that name, so this is rendered as a succession, not a bloodline. Elamite chronology is genuinely uncertain and fragmentary: the early Awan names (Peli through Napilhush) are little more than names in a list, the middle Awan kings (Luhishan, Hishep-ratep) appear in Akkadian sources and are firmer, and Kutik-Inshushinak and the Shimashki kings are firmly historical. Dates are scholarly approximations. (B) THE KINGS OF ASSYRIA (ids prefixed AS_) — the earliest section of the Assyrian King List: the 'seventeen kings who lived in tents' (legendary, uncorroborated by any contemporary record, likely partly invented) down to the first firmly-attested city-kings of Ashur (Puzur-Ashur I through Erishum I, c.2025–1950 BC). Legendary at the top, historical from Puzur-Ashur I down. Each chain is ordered by strictly descending date (earliest BC at top). Sources: Wikipedia 'List of Elamite kings', 'Awan dynasty', 'Shimashki dynasty', 'List of Assyrian kings', 'Puzur-Ashur I', 'Erishum I'; Encyclopaedia Iranica 'Awan'; Potts, The Archaeology of Elam.

a.label:
Peli (Pieli)
c.2400 BC · first named king of the Awan dynasty · legendary / a name in the Susa list

children.label:
Tata (Tari)
c.2380 BC · Awan dynasty · legendary / a name in the Susa list

Ukkutahesh
c.2360 BC · Awan dynasty · legendary / a name in the Susa list

Hishur
c.2350 BC · Awan dynasty · legendary / a name in the Susa list

Napilhush
c.2340 BC · Awan dynasty · legendary / a name in the Susa list

Luhishan (Luhhishan)
c.2325 BC · Awan king named in Sargon of Akkad's inscriptions · historical (firmer)

Hishep-ratep
c.2260 BC · Awan king; fought Naram-Sin of Akkad · historical (firmer)

Kutik-Inshushinak (Puzur-Inshushinak)
c.2100 BC · last and greatest king of Awan; freed Elam from Akkad · historical

Girnamme
c.2037 BC · first king of the Shimashki dynasty; contemporary of Shu-Sin of Ur · historical

Kindattu
c.2000 BC · Shimashki king who sacked Ur and ended the Third Dynasty of Ur · historical

Idattu I (Indattu-Inshushinak)
c.1970 BC · Shimashki king; styled himself 'king of Shimashki and Elam' · historical

a.label:
Tudiya
c.2450 BC · first of the 'seventeen kings who lived in tents' · legendary

children.label:
Adamu
c.2420 BC · second of the kings who lived in tents · legendary

Yangi
c.2400 BC · a king who lived in tents · legendary

Harharu
c.2350 BC · a king who lived in tents · legendary

Didanu
c.2250 BC · a king who lived in tents · legendary

Ushpia
c.2050 BC · traditionally credited with founding the temple of Ashur · legendary (semi-historical)

Apiashal
c.2030 BC · son of Ushpia; last of the kings who lived in tents · legendary

Puzur-Ashur I
c.2025 BC · founder of Ashur as an independent city-state · historical (dynasty founder)

Shalim-ahum
c.2000 BC · son of Puzur-Ashur I; earliest Assyrian ruler attested in a contemporary inscription · historical

Ilu-shuma
c.1975 BC · son of Shalim-ahum; early builder-king of Ashur · historical

Erishum I
c.1950 BC · son of Ilu-shuma; expanded Assyrian trade colonies in Anatolia · historical
